policy: Opportunity packets identify research priority only. No packet authorizes a buy without primary evidence, price zone, invalidation, risk budget, and human review.

packets.name: Dynatrace
packets.whyInteresting: Profitable SaaS platform at 25x FCF with 18.8% revenue growth and $530M annual free cash flow — the closest analog to ServiceNow's profile from a week ago. As AI makes software architectures more complex and distributed, cloud observability becomes mandatory enterprise infrastructure rather than optional tooling. Dynatrace serves the large-enterprise end that Datadog hasn't fully penetrated, and its valuation has reset significantly relative to peers (DDOG trades at 86x FCF, CRWD at 144x).
whyNow: DT: Profitable SaaS platform at 25x FCF with 18.8% revenue growth and $530M annual free cash flow — the closest analog to ServiceNow's profile from a week ago. As AI makes software architectures more complex and distributed, cloud observability becomes mandatory enterprise infrastructure rather than optional tooling. Dynatrace serves the large-enterprise end that Datadog hasn't fully penetrated, and its valuation has reset significantly relative to peers (DDOG trades at 86x FCF, CRWD at 144x).

evidenceRefs.use: internal candidate thesis and current quote context
required next primary-source filing check before promotion

confirmBeforePromotion: Enterprise win rate vs Datadog in large accounts is holding or improving
AI platform expansion (Davis AI) is driving NRR expansion, not just base renewal
FCF conversion remains above 25% of revenue
invalidationQuestions: Risk to invalidate/check: Datadog competition in mid-market and cloud-native accounts
Risk to invalidate/check: Sales cycle elongation in enterprise IT budget freezes
Risk to invalidate/check: Multiple re-compression if growth decelerates below 15%
missingForPromotion: defined add zone and invalidation level

relatedNativeEvents.summary: DT: Profitable SaaS platform at 25x FCF with 18.8% revenue growth and $530M annual free cash flow — the closest analog to ServiceNow's profile from a week ago. packets.name: Beta Technologies
packets.whyInteresting: Asymmetric optionality on aviation electrification: $36M revenue growing 136% YoY, $0.7B market cap. Beta is building the charging infrastructure and power network for electric aircraft before the planes exist at scale — similar positioning to early EV charging networks. The differentiated angle is infrastructure-first rather than aircraft-first. If electric aviation timelines compress, the network effect on charging infrastructure could be substantial. Speculative tier alongside OKLO — not a core position, but a legitimate asymmetric bet with a differentiated thesis.
whyNow: In Opportunity Scout with long_term_macro_fit classification.

evidenceRefs.use: internal candidate thesis and current quote context
required next primary-source filing check before promotion

confirmBeforePromotion: Cash runway extends beyond 24 months or funding round de-risks burn
Aircraft certification progress at partner OEMs (United Airlines, Air New Zealand deals)
Charging network installations are growing, not just contracts signed
invalidationQuestions: Risk to invalidate/check: Pre-commercial: cash burn $313M with only $36M revenue
Risk to invalidate/check: Electric aviation regulatory timelines may push 5-10 years
Risk to invalidate/check: Extreme dilution risk if fundraising at distressed terms
missingForPromotion: defined add zone and invalidation level
packets.sourceBoundary: Local/public/internal evidence only. No web_search, no fresh news causality claim.

packets.name: The Trade Desk
packets.whyInteresting: The cheapest name in the enterprise-SaaS/platform cohort at 12.7x FCF for 18.5% revenue growth — trading at a steep discount because of a significant earnings miss in late 2024 that damaged management credibility. If that was an execution stumble rather than a structural problem, the asymmetry is real: CTV advertising is still growing, programmatic is becoming AI-driven, and TTD is the dominant independent DSP with no platform conflict of interest (unlike Google/Amazon). Evidence-gated: requires confirmation that the miss was one-time before promotion.
whyNow: TTD: The cheapest name in the enterprise-SaaS/platform cohort at 12.7x FCF for 18.5% revenue growth — trading at a steep discount because of a significant earnings miss in late 2024 that damaged management credibility. If that was an execution stumble rather than a structural problem, the asymmetry is real: CTV advertising is still growing, programmatic is becoming AI-driven, and TTD is the dominant independent DSP with no platform conflict of interest (unlike Google/Amazon). Evidence-gated: requires confirmation that the miss was one-time before promotion.

evidenceRefs.use: internal candidate thesis and current quote context
required next primary-source filing check before promotion

confirmBeforePromotion: Q1/Q2 2026 results show execution recovery from late-2024 miss
CTV advertising growth and UID2 adoption trajectory is intact
Management guidance credibility has been re-established
invalidationQuestions: Risk to invalidate/check: Late-2024 earnings miss may signal structural rather than one-time problem
Risk to invalidate/check: Google/Amazon DSP competition in programmatic
Risk to invalidate/check: Advertising cyclicality in macro slowdown
missingForPromotion: defined add zone and invalidation level
